dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8679 IH: To amend title 38, United States Code, to require the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to transmit a veteran’s history of opioid prescriptions to a Community Care health care provider.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Collins (for himself, Mr. Pfluger, Mr. Luttrell, Mr. Babin, Mr. Baird, Mr. Rogers of Kentucky, and Mr. Fuller) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to require the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to transmit a veteran’s history of opioid prescriptions to a Community Care health care provider.

section.header: Transmission of opioid prescription history to Community Care provider

subsection.text: Section 1703C of title 38, United States Code, is amended, in subsection (a), by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The standards established under this subsection shall require the Secretary to transmit a history of opioid prescriptions contained in records of the Department regarding a covered veteran—

subparagraph.text: to a non-Department health care provider through whom the Secretary furnishes health care, pursuant to section 1703 of this title, to such covered veteran; and

subsection.text: Such section is further amended, in subsection (c), by adding at the end the following new paragraphs:

paragraph.text: The term Third Party Administrator has the meaning given such term in section 1703B of this title.
